name: Breadsticks (Plain or Seeded)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups All-purpose flour, or as needed
4 teaspoons Fennel or caraway seeds OR 2 tablespoons Poppy seeds
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the water, sugar, and yeast in a small bowl and let work until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. In a large bowl of electric mixer, combine the yeast mixture, salt, oil, and 1 1/4 cups flour. Beat on medium speed for 4 to 5 minutes.
3. If using an electric mixer, switch to dough hook and on low speed, continue to add the remaining flour as needed to make a medium stiff dough. Increase speed to medium and continue mixing until the dough cleans the bowl completely.
4. Turn the dough into a greased bowl and turn so that the greased side is up. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled in bulk.
5. When proofing is complete, punch down the dough and knead in seeds if desired. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rest for 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Turn out onto a lightly floured working surface and divide into 32 pieces. Place plastic over the dough.
7. Roll one at a time to form a rope about 7 or 8 inches long (they'll be very thin). Place on lightly greased baking sheets and proof once more until doubled.
8. Preheat oven to 325 F.
9. Bake on the middle shelf of the oven until the sticks are nicely browned all over. After 5 minutes, without removing from the oven, spray with water using a trigger-type sprayer or place ice cubes in the bottom of the oven.
10. Repeat after 5 more minutes, then complete baking. Cool on racks and store airtight. Will keep for weeks.
